review.summary: The category decisions and exclusions were reviewed with the project owner and accepted for now.

policy_decisions.summary: Exclude endpoint-agent local CLI operations, installer execution, uninstall, service control, and host-local diagnostics. Cover only API-backed metadata and configuration.

Exclude live telemetry streaming, spout-style pull loops, and firehose receiver behavior. Cover bounded historical events, detections, audit logs, search, replay dry-runs, and case telemetry references.
Keep dry-run replay. Defer non-dry-run replay until it has a dedicated preview/confirm design with blast-radius summary.
Exclude shell-style cloud CLI execution. Cover deterministic API configuration and state surfaces with redaction and preview/confirm.
Do not run the MCP as an output receiver or telemetry sink. Output configuration can be covered with preview/confirm.
Do not proxy LimaCharlie's hosted MCP. This server should remain a reviewable local API-only implementation.
Do not wrap the LimaCharlie CLI. The MCP implementation must use API surfaces directly.
Do not mirror static documentation into MCP tools. Cover compact structured reference data where upstream exposes it.
Exclude broad AI chat/generation wrappers. Cover deterministic governance, usage, sessions, skills, and memory state.

decision_values.cover: MCP should provide direct tool coverage.
decision_values.cover_bounded: MCP should cover the category only through bounded reads or preview/confirm mutations.
decision_values.defer: MCP should not implement this until a safer typed design is reviewed.
decision_values.exclude: MCP should intentionally not expose this capability.
decision_values.reference_only: No MCP API surface is expected; keep as docs or catalog metadata.

categories.title: Getting Started
categories.reason: Onboarding concepts are documentation and setup flow, not LimaCharlie API tools. Auth and org discovery are covered in platform tools.

categories.title: Sensors, Adapters, And Deployment
categories.reason: Inventory, tags, version policy, installation keys, payload metadata, and adapter configuration are useful to agents when bounded. Local installer execution and endpoint-agent CLI operation are not MCP responsibilities.

excluded_capabilities.reason: Installing or controlling endpoint agents locally is host operations work, not a bounded LimaCharlie API MCP tool.
Running installers, uninstallers, or service-control operations is host-mutating and OS-specific; use MDM, RMM, scripts, or a separate host-management tool.
Host-local diagnostics through endpoint-agent CLI would require local process and filesystem access outside the LimaCharlie API boundary.

categories.title: Detection And Response
categories.reason: D&R rules, false positives, replay dry-runs, YARA, managed content, MITRE coverage, and response actions are in scope when reads are bounded and writes use preview/confirm.

deferred_capabilities.reason: Non-dry-run replay can create operational effects and needs a dedicated preview/confirm design before exposure.

categories.title: Data And Queries
categories.reason: Historical events, detections, LCQL, saved queries, artifacts, payloads, audit logs, and ARLs are useful when bounded by explicit limits, cursors, and time windows.

categories.title: Integrations, Outputs, Extensions, And Services
categories.reason: Outputs, extension subscriptions, service requests, cloud/external adapters, feedback, vulnerability reporting, config sync, and API integration config are in scope with bounded reads, redaction, and preview/confirm writes.

excluded_capabilities.reason: The MCP runtime should not act as a live output receiver or telemetry sink.
Cloud CLI extensions are execution environments and credential-heavy operational workflows, not deterministic API tools.

categories.title: Developer Guide, SDKs, CLI, And Hosted MCP
categories.reason: SDK and CLI docs inform parity checks, but this server should remain API-only and should not wrap CLI commands. Hosted MCP docs describe LimaCharlie's own remote MCP, not this local stdio runtime.

excluded_capabilities.reason: The local MCP must use LimaCharlie APIs directly and must not shell out to lc CLI.
Proxying the hosted LimaCharlie MCP would duplicate trust and auth surfaces instead of making a reviewable local API implementation.

categories.title: Administration, Access, Billing, And Config Hive
categories.reason: Administration is a core MCP surface when org scope is explicit, secrets are redacted, and writes use preview/confirm.

categories.title: Reference Material
categories.reason: Reference pages should become compact discovery/read tools only where the upstream exposes structured API data. Static docs remain reference-only.

excluded_capabilities.reason: The MCP should not mirror static documentation pages when the docs site is the better source.

categories.title: AI Sessions
categories.reason: Governance, usage, memory, and skill state are in scope. Starting broad AI generation/chat sessions is intentionally not a parity target because the user is already operating through an agent.

excluded_capabilities.reason: Chat/generation wrappers are redundant and risk agent-on-agent confusion; deterministic governance and state tools are preferred.

categories.title: Posture Review And Tuning
categories.reason: Aggregate expert-review tools are in scope when they summarize bounded reads, preserve source-level permission failures, and do not mutate LimaCharlie state.

categories.title: Release Notes
categories.reason: Release notes are documentation, not an API tool category. They are useful as an audit input when deciding whether categories have changed.
